zh.lifeSummary: 當Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein於1229年4月13日出生在德國巴登可萊斯 海德爾格時，他的父親Otto II von Bayern23歲，母親Agnes von Braunschweig28歲。他於0002年8月與Maria van Brabant結婚。他去世於1294年2月2日在他的家鄉享年64歲，埋葬在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 費爾斯騰費爾德布盧可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein于1229年4月13日出生在德国巴登可萊斯 海德爾格时，他的父亲Otto II von Bayern23岁，母亲Agnes von Braunschweig28岁。他于0002年8月与Maria van Brabant结婚。他去世于1294年2月2日在他的家乡享年64岁，埋葬在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 費爾斯騰費爾德布盧可。

nameMeanings.name: Herzog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Heinz, Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang, Franz, Arno, Ernst, Hildegarde, Reinhold.

German: from the Middle High German title of nobility herzoge ‘duke’ (Old High German herizoho, from heri ‘army’ + ziohan ‘to lead’, a calque of the Byzantine title stratēlatēs ‘general, commander’, from Greek stratos ‘army’ + elaunein ‘to lead’). The name is unlikely to refer to descent from an actual duke; it is normally an occupational name for the servant of a duke or a nickname for one who put on the airs and graces of a duke. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below). Compare Hertzog .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Herzog ‘duke’ (see 1 above). Compare Graf and Kaiser .

variants.name: Hartsook
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herceg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Croatian Stanko.

Croatian: nickname from herceg ‘duke’, a title of the medieval feudal lords of Herzegovina (‘duke's land’, a region in Bosnia and Herzegovina), derived from the Hungarian form of German Herzog . Compare Erceg .

variants.name: Herczeg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Imre, Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Ferenc, Gaza, Istvan, Janos.
Hungarian form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herzig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Fritzi, Hans, Horst, Markus, Monika.
German (eastern and central Germany and Silesia, Switzerland): variant of   Herzog  .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names composed with hard ‘strong, hardy’, for instance Herto.

variants.name: Hertzog
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Herzog and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Hertzog is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Hartzog
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog   1.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsock
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsoe
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsough
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Pfalzgraf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel.

German: status name for the governor or judge at an imperial court, or for someone in the service of such a dignitary, from Middle High German phalenzgrāve. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Pfaltzgraff .

variants.name: Pfaltzgraff
meanings: Altered form of German   Pfalzgraf  .

variants.name: Palsgrove
meanings: Americanized form of German   Pfalzgraf  .

variants.name: Polsgrove
meanings: Americanized form of German   Pfalzgraf  . Compare   Palsgrove  .

variants.name: Pfalz
meanings: German:
habitational name from the Palatinate (German Pfalz), a region in southwestern Germany.

nameMeanings.name: bei
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Italian (Bèi): regional variant of Belli . The name is found especially in Perugia province.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 貝, meaning ‘cowrie’ or ‘shell’ in Chinese: (i) from the placename Bei Qiu (貝丘), originally a fief (located in Hebei province) in the state of Jin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) simplified form of Bei (郥), the name of an ancient state (located in Shandong province).
Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 秘 (also pronounced as Bi or Mi in Mandarin in some local regions), see   Bi   3.

variants.name: Belli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Aldo, Remo, Caesar, Dino, Guido, Antonio, Edo, Elio, Enrico, Ferdinando, Guilio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Bello  .

nameMeanings.name: Rhein
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Arno, Elke, Erna, Helmut, Horst, Kurt.
German:

topographic name for someone who lived by the Rhine river, Middle High German Rīn, which is first recorded in the Roman period in the form Rhenus; it may be derived from a Celtic element meaning ‘to flow’.

variants.name: Rhine
meanings: German: name from the common river name Rhin.
Americanized form of German   Rhein  .

variants.name: Rhyne
meanings: Possibly also an Americanized form of Swiss German Rhyn: topographic name for someone living on the Rhine river, Middle High German Rīn.
Americanized form of German   Rhein   or   Rein  , or perhaps of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

nameMeanings.name: Ludwig
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from hlud ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’; an ancient royal and imperial name. See Louis .

variants.name: Louis
meanings: name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hlōd ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’. It was very common in French royal and noble families. Louis I ( 778–840 ) was the son of Charlemagne, who ruled as both King of France and Holy Roman Emperor. Altogether, the name was borne by sixteen kings of France up to the French Revolution, in which Louis XVI perished. Louis XIV, ‘the Sun King’ ( 1638–1715 ), reigned for seventy-two years ( 1643–1715 ), presiding in the middle part of his reign over a period of unparalleled French power and prosperity. In modern times Louis is also found in the English-speaking world (usually pronounced ‘loo-ee’). In Britain the Anglicized form Lewis is rather more common, whereas in America the reverse is true.

nameMeanings.name: II.,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant of   Dev .

nameMeanings.name: Strenge,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt.
German:
nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German strenge ‘strong, brave, hardy’.

en.lifeSummary: When Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein was born on 13 April 1229, in Heidelberg, Baden, Germany, his father, Otto II von Bayern, was 23 and his mother, Agnes von Braunschweig, was 28. He married Maria van Brabant in August 0002. He died on 2 February 1294, in his hometown, at the age of 64, and was buried in Fürstenfeldbruck, Bavaria, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein est né le 13 avril 1229, à Kreis Heidelberg, Baden, Allemagne, son père, Otto II von Bayern, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Agnes von Braunschweig, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Maria van Brabant en août 0002. Il est décédé le 2 février 1294, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein am 13. April 1229 in Kreis Heidelberg, Baden, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Otto II von Bayern 23 und seine Mutter Agnes von Braunschweig 28. Er hat im August 0002 Maria van Brabant geheiratet. Er starb am 2. Februar 1294 im Alter von 64 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein è nato il il 13 aprile 1229 nel Kreis Heidelberg, Baden, Germania, il suo padre, Otto II von Bayern, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Agnes von Braunschweig, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Maria van Brabant nel agosto 0002. Morì il 2 febbraio 1294, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolto a Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rheinが1229年4月13日にドイツバデンクレイス ヘイデルバーグで生まれた時，父Otto II von Bayernは23歳，母Agnes von Braunschweigは28歳であった。0002年8月にMaria van Brabantと結婚した。彼は1294年2月2日に故郷で64歳で死去し，ドイツバイエルンランドクレイス フuルステンフエルドブルックにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein(이)가 1229년 4월 13일 독일 바든 크레이스 헤이들버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Otto II von Bayern(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes von Braunschweig(은)는 28세였다. 그는 0002년 8월 Maria van Brabant(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1294년 2월 2일 그의 고향에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 프u르스펠드브럭에 묻혔다.

mn.13 April 1229–2 February 1294: 1229 оны Дөрөвдүгээр сарын 13–1294 оны Хоёрдугаар сарын 2

pt.13 April 1229–2 February 1294: 13 de abril de 1229–2 de fevereiro de 1294
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein nasceu em 13 de abril de 1229, em Kreis Heidelberg, Baden, Alemanha, seu pai, Otto II von Bayern, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Agnes von Braunschweig, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Maria van Brabant em agosto de 0002. Ele faleceu em 2 de fevereiro de 1294, em sua cidade natal, com 64 anos, e foi sepultado em Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein родился 13 апреля 1229 г. в местности крис хиделбирг, баден, Германия, его отцу по имени Otto II von Bayern было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Agnes von Braunschweig, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Maria van Brabant август 0002 г. Он умер 2 февраля 1294 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности ландкрис фuрстенфелдбрукк, беиирн, Германия.

es.13 April 1229–2 February 1294: 13 de abril de 1229–2 de febrero de 1294
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Ludwig II., der Strenge, Herzog von Oberbayern, Pfalzgraf bei Rhein nació el 13 de abril de 1229, en Kreis Heidelberg, Baden, Alemania, su papá, Otto II von Bayern, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Agnes von Braunschweig, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Maria van Brabant en agosto de 0002. Murió el 2 de febrero de 1294, en su pueblo, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultado en Landkreis Fürstenfeldbruck, Bayern, Alemania.
